task_completion.description: Verify that the brain MRI (FLAIR sequence) is loaded and displayed in the axial view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewer shows a grayscale cross-section of a brain. The background is black, and the brain tissue is gray.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent has navigated to a slice where the tumor is clearly visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct bright/hyperintense (white) region is visible within the brain parenchyma, contrasting with the darker gray healthy tissue.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Markups module is accessed or the point placement tool is active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Markups' module is visible in the sidebar, or the mouse cursor has changed to a crosshair/placement icon, or the 'Place a Control Point' button is highlighted.

task_completion.description: Verify that a fiducial marker has been placed on the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A colored dot, crosshair, or star labeled (e.g., 'F-1', '1') is overlaid on the MRI image.

task_completion.description: Verify that the fiducial marker is placed within the boundaries of the tumor mass, ideally near the center.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The marker is located inside the bright white tumor region, not in the surrounding gray brain tissue or black background.

task_completion.description: Verify that the selected slice appears to show the tumor at its maximum cross-sectional area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible tumor area is large and well-defined, appearing as a substantial mass rather than a small sliver or edge. The slice choice reflects a visual maximum compared to potential smaller slices.

integrity.description: Verify the agent is using the software GUI (sliders, mouse clicks) to navigate and place points.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard 3D Slicer interface elements (slice views, module panel) being used.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use the Python console to programmatically solve the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python console is either closed, hidden, or does not contain code calculating tumor centroids or max areas.

integrity.description: Verify the agent navigated through slices to find the max area rather than jumping to a single coordinate immediately.
integrity.visual_evidence: Multiple screenshots or history implies scrolling/checking different slices before final placement.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dataset: BraTS 2021. Modality: MRI FLAIR. Visual Target: The tumor appears as a hyperintense (bright white) region against darker brain tissue. The objective is to identify the slice where this white region has the largest surface area.
